skill.name: post-impl-docs
skill.description: Updates repository documentation after implementation by reviewing code changes, README impact, changelog entries, docs references, and inline docstrings.
skill.summary: Keep README files, changelogs, docs, and docstrings aligned with recent code changes.
skill.author: Consiliency

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, environment, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found they are markdown examples or documentation workflow instructions. No executable scripts, prompt injection attempts, credential exfiltration, or malicious network behavior were found; the main residual risk is that the skill intentionally asks agents to inspect diffs and edit repository documentation.

low_findings.title: Documentation Skill Can Modify Repository Files
low_findings.description: The skill is designed to update README, CHANGELOG, docs folder content, and inline docstrings. This is expected behavior for the skill, but users should review generated documentation diffs before committing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The core instructions explicitly require documentation updates in repository files. This is a real capability but matches the declared purpose and is not malicious.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Local Workflow Examples
low_findings.description: The flagged command patterns are git, grep, changelog, and doctest examples used to inspect changes or verify documentation. They are not hidden executable code and do not include untrusted shell interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review confirmed these are fenced markdown examples and local development commands. No evidence found of command injection or covert execution.

low_findings.title: Network and Credential Alerts Are Placeholder Documentation
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs reference public documentation and example release comparison links. The API key string is a placeholder in a README example, not code that reads or transmits secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs and key-like text appear inside explanatory documentation examples. No evidence found of outbound requests, environment reads, or credential exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits appear to be caused by documentation terms, markdown filenames, examples, or ordinary words. No hashing, encryption, signing, or security-sensitive cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are documentation and examples rather than executable cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar algorithms being used for security.

low_findings.title: Critical Heuristic Combination Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The analyzer combined command examples, links, and placeholder credential text into a critical heuristic. Semantic review found no prompt injection, no malicious intent, and no code path that combines command execution with network exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious signals exist only as documentation examples across markdown files. No evidence found that they are connected into a data exfiltration or persistence workflow.

content.value_statement: Code changes often leave README files, changelogs, and docstrings stale. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused documentation updates after implementation work.
actual_capabilities: Reviews git diffs to identify documentation impact after code changes.
Updates README sections for new public features, APIs, dependencies, and breaking changes.
Creates changelog entries in Conventional Changelog, Keep a Changelog, or simple formats.
Finds docs folder references to changed functions, classes, and moved files.
Updates inline docstrings for changed parameters, return values, exceptions, and behavior.
Produces a documentation update report or a no-change report.
limitations: It depends on available git history or diffs to understand recent implementation changes.
It does not prove examples compile unless the user also runs project tests or doctests.
It cannot infer private product decisions that are not visible in the repository.
It may skip internal refactors when there is no user-facing documentation impact.

use_cases.title: Refresh docs after a feature merge
use_cases.description: Update README, changelog, and API references after adding a user-facing capability.

use_cases.title: Prepare release documentation
use_cases.description: Convert completed implementation work into clear changelog entries before release review.

use_cases.title: Maintain inline API documentation
use_cases.description: Align docstrings with changed parameters, returns, exceptions, and examples.

prompt_templates.title: Basic documentation refresh
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after a small implementation task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the latest code changes and update any README, changelog, docs folder, or docstring content that is now stale.

prompt_templates.title: README and changelog update
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after adding a public feature or API.
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the implementation diff. Update README usage or API sections and add a concise changelog entry in the project format.

prompt_templates.title: Breaking change documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after changing public behavior or signatures.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all documentation affected by this breaking change. Add migration guidance, update examples, and record the change in the changelog.

prompt_templates.title: Full documentation audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before opening a pull request or release branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the repository documentation against the completed implementation. Update README, CHANGELOG, docs references, and docstrings, then summarize what changed and what did not need changes.

output_examples.input: A new public authentication endpoint was implemented.
output_examples.output: README gains an authentication usage section, CHANGELOG records a feature entry, and API docs reference the new endpoint.

output_examples.input: A function signature changed and raises a new validation error.
output_examples.output: Docstrings are updated with the new parameter contract, return behavior, and validation error documentation.

output_examples.input: The change was an internal refactor with no public behavior change.
output_examples.output: No user-facing documentation updates are made, and the report explains why documentation impact is absent.

best_practices: Run the skill after implementation but before pull request review.
Review generated examples against real project behavior before committing.
Keep changelog entries user-facing and avoid internal implementation details.
anti_patterns: Do not update README content for purely internal refactors.
Do not add vague changelog entries such as miscellaneous fixes.
Do not change docstrings without checking the actual code behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill edit source code?
faq.answer: It focuses on documentation files and inline docstrings. It should not change implementation behavior.

faq.question: Does it support multiple changelog formats?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for Keep a Changelog, Conventional Changelog, and simple changelog formats.

faq.question: When should I run it?
faq.answer: Run it after implementation work and before pull request or release review.

faq.question: Can it update documentation in the docs folder?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the agent to find references to changed symbols and update stale documentation.

faq.question: Will it verify examples automatically?
faq.answer: It recommends doctest and related checks, but verification depends on the project tools available.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: The skill contains no exfiltration instructions, but users should still review documentation changes before committing.
